description:
Create a new online Prisma Postgres database.
      Specify a name that makes sense to the user - maybe the name of the project they are working on.
      Specify a region that makes sense for the user. Pick between these three options: us-east-1, eu-west-3, ap-northeast-1. If you are unsure, pick us-east-1.
      Provide the current working directory of the users project. This should be the top level directory of the project.
      If the response idicates that you have reached the workspace plan limit, you should instruct the user to do one of these things:
      - If they want to connect to an existing database, they should go to console.prisma.io and copy the connection string
      - If they want to upgrade their plan, they should go to console.prisma.io and upgrade their plan in order to be able to create more databases
      - If they want to delete a database they no longer need, they should go to console.prisma.io and delete the database project

description: Advanced file and code search tool with comprehensive filtering and matching capabilities. Searches for patterns in files within allowed directories with support for regex patterns, file type filtering, size constraints, date filtering, and content preprocessing. When called without arguments, searches for common patterns in the current directory. Supports excluding comments and string literals for cleaner code searches. Results can be formatted as text, JSON, or structured output with configurable sorting and grouping options.

pattern.description: Search pattern - can be literal text or regex depending on regexMode. Defaults to searching for common file types if not specified

searchPath.description: Directory path to search in. Must be within allowed directories. Defaults to first allowed directory if not specified

extensions.description: Array of file extensions to include (e.g., ['.js', '.ts', '.py']). Include the dot prefix

excludeExtensions.description: Array of file extensions to exclude

excludePatterns.description: Array of filename patterns to exclude (supports simple wildcards)

regexMode.description: Whether to treat pattern as a regular expression

caseSensitive.description: Whether search should be case sensitive

wordBoundary.description: Whether to match whole words only

multiline.description: Whether to enable multiline regex matching

maxDepth.description: Maximum directory recursion depth. Unlimited if not specified

includeBinary.description: Whether to search in binary files

modifiedAfter.description: Only include files modified after this date (ISO 8601 format)

modifiedBefore.description: Only include files modified before this date (ISO 8601 format)

snippetLength.description: Length of text snippet around matches

maxResults.description: Maximum number of match results to return

groupByFile.description: Whether to group results by file

excludeComments.description: Whether to exclude comments from search (language-aware)

excludeStrings.description: Whether to exclude string literals from search

description: Generate an SVG from a text prompt using Recraft model
full_card:
{
  "name": "generate_svg",
  "description": "Generate an SVG from a text prompt using Recraft model",
  "parameters": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "prompt": {
        "type": "string",
        "minLength": 1.0,
        "description": "Prompt for generated SVG"
      },
      "size": {
        "type": "string",
        "enum": [
          "1024x1024",
          "1365x1024",
          "1024x1365",
          "1536x1024",
          "1024x1536",
          "1820x1024",
          "1024x1820",
          "1024x2048",
          "2048x1024",
          "1434x1024",
          "1024x1434",
          "1024x1280",
          "1280x1024",
          "1024x1707",
          "1707x1024"
        ],
        "default": "1024x1024",
        "description": "Size of the generated SVG"
      },
      "style": {
        "type": "string",
        "enum": [
          "any",
          "engraving",
          "line_art",
          "line_circuit",
          "linocut"
        ],
        "default": "any",
        "description": "Style of the generated image."
      }
    },
    "required": [
      "prompt"
    ],
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#"
  }
}
compact_card:
Tool: generate_svg
Use: Generate an SVG from a text prompt using Recraft model
Required args: prompt: string (Prompt for generated SVG)
Optional args: size: string (Size of the generated SVG); style: string (Style of the generated image.)

description: Package a local code directory into a consolidated XML file for AI analysis. This tool analyzes the codebase structure, extracts relevant code content, and generates a comprehensive report including metrics, file tree, and formatted code content. Supports Tree-sitter compression for efficient token usage.

compress.description: Enable Tree-sitter compression to extract essential code signatures and structure while removing implementation details. Reduces token usage by ~70% while preserving semantic meaning. Generally not needed since grep_repomix_output allows incremental content retrieval. Use only when you specifically need the entire codebase content for large repositories (default: false).

includePatterns.description: Specify files to include using fast-glob patterns. Multiple patterns can be comma-separated (e.g., "**/*.{js,ts}", "src/**,docs/**"). Only matching files will be processed. Useful for focusing on specific parts of the codebase.

ignorePatterns.description: Specify additional files to exclude using fast-glob patterns. Multiple patterns can be comma-separated (e.g., "test/**,*.spec.js", "node_modules/**,dist/**"). These patterns supplement .gitignore and built-in exclusions.

topFilesLength.description: Number of largest files by size to display in the metrics summary for codebase analysis (default: 10)

Optional args: compress: boolean (Enable Tree-sitter compression to extract essential code signatures and structure while removing implementation details. Reduces token usage by ~70% while preserving semantic meaning. Generally not needed since grep_repomix_output allows incremental content retrieval. Use only when you specifically need the entire codebase content for large repositories (default: false).); includePatterns: string (Specify files to include using fast-glob patterns. Multiple patterns can be comma-separated (e.g., "**/*.{js,ts}", "src/**,docs/**"). Only matching files will be processed. Useful for focusing on specific parts of the codebase.); ignorePatterns: string (Specify additional files to exclude using fast-glob patterns. Multiple patterns can be comma-separated (e.g., "test/**,*.spec.js", "node_modules/**,dist/**"). These patterns supplement .gitignore and built-in exclusions.); topFilesLength: number (Number of largest files by size to display in the metrics summary for codebase analysis (default: 10))

Reduces token usage by ~70% while preserving semantic meaning. Generally not needed since grep_repomix_output allows incremental content retrieval. Use only when you specifically need the entire codebase content for large repositories (default: false).

includePatterns.description: Specify files to include using fast-glob patterns. Multiple patterns can be comma-separated (e.g., "**/*.{js,ts}", "src/**,docs/**"). Only matching files will be processed. Useful for focusing on specific parts of the codebase.

description:
Query records from any Salesforce object using SOQL, including relationship queries.

Examples:
1. Parent-to-child query (e.g., Account with Contacts):
   - objectName: "Account"
   - fields: ["Name", "(SELECT Id, FirstName, LastName FROM Contacts)"]

2. Child-to-parent query (e.g., Contact with Account details):
   - objectName: "Contact"
   - fields: ["FirstName", "LastName", "Account.Name", "Account.Industry"]

3. Multiple level query (e.g., Contact -> Account -> Owner):
   - objectName: "Contact"
   - fields: ["Name", "Account.Name", "Account.Owner.Name"]

4. Related object filtering:
   - objectName: "Contact"
   - fields: ["Name", "Account.Name"]
   - whereClause: "Account.Industry = 'Technology'"

Note: When using relationship fields:
- Use dot notation for parent relationships (e.g., "Account.Name")
- Use subqueries in parentheses for child relationships (e.g., "(SELECT Id FROM Contacts)")
- Custom relationship fields end in "__r" (e.g., "CustomObject__r.Name")

objectName.description: API name of the object to query

fields.description: List of fields to retrieve, including relationship fields

whereClause.description: WHERE clause, can include conditions on related objects

orderBy.description: ORDER BY clause, can include fields from related objects

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return

Required args: objectName: string (API name of the object to query); fields: array (List of fields to retrieve, including relationship fields)

The user will then be asked to pick the documents they want to ingest. This is useful if you want to update the connection to a different source or if you want to update the clientRedirectUrl or if you want to pick a different or new set of documents.

description: A powerful web search tool that provides comprehensive, real-time results using Tavily's AI search engine. Returns relevant web content with customizable parameters for result count, content type, and domain filtering. Ideal for gathering current information, news, and detailed web content analysis.

query.description: Search query

search_depth.description: The depth of the search. It can be 'basic' or 'advanced'

topic.description: The category of the search. This will determine which of our agents will be used for the search

days.description: The number of days back from the current date to include in the search results. This specifies the time frame of data to be retrieved. Please note that this feature is only available when using the 'news' search topic

time_range.description: The time range back from the current date to include in the search results. This feature is available for both 'general' and 'news' search topics

max_results.description: The maximum number of search results to return

include_images.description: Include a list of query-related images in the response

include_image_descriptions.description: Include a list of query-related images and their descriptions in the response

include_raw_content.description: Include the cleaned and parsed HTML content of each search result

include_domains.description: A list of domains to specifically include in the search results, if the user asks to search on specific sites set this to the domain of the site

exclude_domains.description: List of domains to specifically exclude, if the user asks to exclude a domain set this to the domain of the site

description: Create a meeting

start_time.description: The meeting's start time. This supports local time and GMT formats.To set a meeting's start time in GMT, use the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ date-time format. For example, 2020-03-31T12:02:00Z. To set a meeting's start time using a specific timezone, use the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss date-time format and specify the timezone ID in the timezone field. If you do not specify a timezone, the timezone value defaults to your Zoom account's timezone. You can also use UTC for the timezone value. Note: If no start_time is set for a scheduled meeting, the start_time is set at the current time and the meeting type changes to an instant meeting, which expires after 30 days. current time is 2025-06-06T15:48:29.724Z.

timezone.description: Timezone for the meeting's start time. The Current timezone is Asia/Shanghai.

To set a meeting's start time using a specific timezone, use the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss date-time format and specify the timezone ID in the timezone field. If you do not specify a timezone, the timezone value defaults to your Zoom account's timezone. You can also use UTC for the timezone value. Note: If no start_time is set for a scheduled meeting, the start_time is set at the current time and the meeting type changes to an instant meeting, which expires after 30 days. current time is 2025-06-06T15:48:29.724Z.); timezone: string (Timezone for the meeting's start time.
